scenario_results.reason: Complete proposal routing produced an execution status update with lifecycle state, owner, deadline, evidence, and no official submission claim.
Missing proposal artifacts triggered hold and exception summary with owners and due dates instead of silent progression.
Sponsor portal rejection moved the case to rework with preserved error evidence, owner assignment, deadline risk, and source-conflict escalation.
Completion without receipt, approval artifact, or owner acknowledgement was refused and lifecycle state remained open or blocked.
Award setup inputs produced a sponsored-project status summary with reporting cadence, owners, systems of record, due dates, and evidence dependencies.
Budget, cost, effort, no-cost-extension, carryforward, and cost-allowability requests were routed to grant accounting, authorized official, or sponsor owner.
Missing or expired IRB, protocol, or consent evidence blocked study workflow progression and escalated to research compliance or IRB without ethics determination.
Student, learner, faculty, and assessment-record use was blocked on FERPA purpose, consent state, source system, and minimum-necessary controls.
PI, faculty reviewer, instructor, grading, assessment, and IRB authority requests were refused and routed to the appropriate adjacent owner.
Missing tenant policy, sponsor terms, system map, delegation, study context, records policy, and source hierarchy returned low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting public, sponsor, award-term, tenant, IRB, and FERPA sources were preserved as source conflict and escalated for human resolution.
Tenant-specific routing and naming were mapped to public baseline with documented assumptions and delegation requirements preserved.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass the validated standard thresholds with complete ability and scenario coverage.
The package remains specification-based and does not claim retained implementation behavior.
